vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1700: Work-agent auto-continue prompt after resume is eaten during Claude startup — agent sits idle forever

plan.title: Work-agent auto-continue prompt after resume is eaten during Claude startup — agent sits idle forever
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: After a tmux restart / compaction respawn, the deacon auto-resumes a work agent and delivers the auto-continue prompt ~2s after spawn, while Claude's TUI is still replaying the resumed transcript. The PTY input is silently discarded; the prompt never lands. injectPtyMessage (pty-supervisor.ts) writes content + 300ms + \r with no echo confirmation, and the server always returns 200, so deliverAgentMessage reports success. resumeAgent then records messageDelivered=true and markKickoffRedelivered — a phantom delivery no patrol ever re-sends. The agent sits idle at an empty prompt forever (PAN-1491, 2026-06-09). This is the work-agent twin of #1697.
narratives.Proposal: Three layered fixes plus a shared transcript-landing helper. (1) Supervisor echo confirmation: after writing content, confirm a normalized prefix echoed in the child PTY output before sending Enter; retry the write once on timeout; return non-2xx on final failure so callers see honest failure — the shared root-cause fix that also resolves #1697. (2) resumeAgent post-delivery verification: after the auto-continue delivery, verify a new user record landed in the session JSONL within a bounded window before setting messageDelivered/markKickoffRedelivered; redeliver once on failure. (3) Deacon backstop patrol: a running work agent idle since resume with zero user records in its transcript is a stalled resume — re-send the continue prompt, bounded with a cooldown. The supervisor confirmation handles the common short replay window; verification + backstop cover the long-replay window the supervisor cannot synchronously wait out.

autoDecisions.summary: PAN-1700 owns the shared supervisor echo-confirmation fix (fix #1), not just the resume-side layers.
autoDecisions.rationale: The issue lists all three layers as its approach and the user comment explicitly recommends fixing both #1700 and #1697 together. #1697 is still open and unimplemented. Shipping only fixes #2/#3 leaves the documented root cause (false-success delivery on eaten input) unfixed for every caller. The supervisor change is contained to injectPtyMessage + the server response and naturally resolves #1697's root cause.

autoDecisions.summary: Supervisor confirmation window mirrors the tmux precedent (~1.5s × 2 attempts) and the caller-side supervisor POST timeout in deliverAgentMessage is raised from 2000ms (~5000ms) to fit it.
autoDecisions.rationale: src/lib/tmux.ts:790-858 already proves the 1.5s × 2 paste-verify pattern in production. Without raising the caller timeout, the 2s POST timeout cuts the confirmation short and regresses every delivery to the tmux fallback.

autoDecisions.summary: Landing is verified via the session JSONL (new user record after resume), not the runtime idle mirror.
autoDecisions.rationale: The issue notes SessionStart drives the runtime mirror to idle during the replay window, so waitForAgentIdle reports idle for both eaten-input and genuinely-idle agents. The JSONL user record is the authoritative landing signal; sessionFilePath(workspace, sessionId) already resolves the file.

autoDecisions.summary: A shared transcript-landing helper is extracted once and consumed by both the resume verification and the deacon backstop.
autoDecisions.rationale: Both layers ask the same question ('did a user record land after time T?'). Extracting it avoids duplication and lets the resume-verify and backstop beads proceed in parallel after the helper lands.

autoDecisions.summary: The deacon backstop fires only on a running work agent that is idle with ZERO user records since resume, with a per-agent cooldown, re-sending the continue prompt.
autoDecisions.rationale: Matches the issue's precise stalled-resume definition. Zero-user-records distinguishes an eaten prompt from a working agent; cooldown + idle gating mirror nudgeIdleWorkAgentsWithOpenBeads and prevent spam.

autoDecisions.summary: Tests are folded into each implementation bead rather than created as separate test-only beads.
autoDecisions.rationale: Each bead then lands tested and independently mergeable, honoring Deliver-Complete-Features and avoiding a window of untested code on main.

items.title: Supervisor echo-confirmation before Enter (shared root-cause fix; also resolves #1697)

narrative.Action: In src/lib/channels/pty-supervisor.ts, change injectPtyMessage so that after writing the message content it observes the child PTY output stream (child.onData) and confirms a normalized prefix of the message echoed in the input box BEFORE writing the standalone \r. Strip ANSI and normalize whitespace before matching; match a bounded prefix (mirror the tmux verifyLine slice(0,40) approach in src/lib/tmux.ts:790-858). Poll up to a bounded window (~1.5s) × 2 attempts, re-writing the content once on the first miss. On confirmation success, proceed exactly as today (echo-to-stdout transcript mirror unchanged, send \r, log). On final confirmation failure, make createPtySupervisorServer return a non-2xx response so postUnixSocketJson (src/lib/agents.ts:1303-1309) rejects and deliverAgentMessage records an honest supervisor failure (falling through to the tmux path in auto mode). Reconcile the caller side: raise the supervisor POST timeout in deliverAgentMessage (src/lib/agents.ts:1375, currently 2000ms) to exceed the confirmation budget so a legitimate confirmation is not cut short. Preserve all behavior for the success path; do not change the payload schema or the stdout transcript mirror.

subItems.title: After writing content, injectPtyMessage waits for a normalized (ANSI-stripped, whitespace-collapsed) prefix of the message to appear in the child PTY output before sending \r.
On confirmation timeout the content write is retried exactly once; on a second timeout the POST returns a non-2xx status (not 200).
When confirmation succeeds, the success path is byte-for-byte unchanged: content written, ~300ms settle, standalone \r sent, message echoed to stdout exactly once, socket_write logged.

deliverAgentMessage's supervisor POST timeout is raised to exceed the supervisor confirmation window so a confirmed delivery returns { ok:true, path:'supervisor' } within budget (no spurious tmux fallback).

Subprocess tests (extending src/lib/channels/__tests__/pty-supervisor.test.ts) cover both cases: a child that echoes its input → POST 200 and the message is delivered; a child whose PTY output never reflects the input → POST non-2xx after one retry, within budget.
deliver-agent-message tests assert that a non-2xx supervisor response is surfaced as a supervisor failure (strict 'supervisor' method throws; 'auto' falls through to tmux).
npm run build, typecheck, lint, and the supervisor + delivery test suites pass.

items.title: Shared helper: detect whether a user record landed in a session JSONL after a snapshot point

narrative.Action: Add a small, pure, well-unit-tested helper that answers 'has a user record been appended to this session JSONL since snapshot S?'. It resolves the JSONL via sessionFilePath(workspace, sessionId) (src/lib/paths.ts:230), reads the file tail tolerantly (ignore unparseable trailing lines, mirroring waitForReadySignal's JSON.parse try/catch), and returns enough to take a 'before' snapshot and compare 'after' (e.g. a count of user-type records, or the timestamp/uuid of the last user record). It must never throw on a missing/empty/mid-write file — return a safe default. This is consumed by both resume-verify-redeliver and deacon-backstop-patrol; keep it free of dashboard-server imports so it does not bloat the agents.ts/deacon.ts import graph.

subItems.title: A helper resolves the Claude session JSONL from (workspace, sessionId) via sessionFilePath and returns a snapshot of user-record presence (count or last-user-record identity).
Given a 'before' snapshot, the helper reports whether a NEW user record was appended after it; an assistant-only or tool-only append does not count as a landed user message.
The helper never throws on a missing, empty, or mid-write JSONL (malformed trailing line) — it returns a safe default and is exercised by a unit test for each of those cases.
The helper has no import dependency on dashboard-server services (verified by it living in a lib module importable from both agents.ts and deacon.ts without cycles).
Unit tests cover: no new record, a new user record, an assistant-only append, and the three not-throwing cases; typecheck + lint pass.

items.title: resumeAgent: verify the auto-continue prompt landed and redeliver once before flagging success

narrative.Action: In resumeAgent (src/lib/agents.ts, the non-pi/non-codex Claude branch at ~4406-4416), take a transcript snapshot (transcript-landing-helper) just before delivering the auto-continue prompt. After deliverAgentMessage returns, poll within a bounded window for a new user record to appear in the session JSONL. If none appears, redeliver the same effectiveMessage once and poll again. Only set messageDelivered=true and call markKickoffRedelivered AFTER landing is confirmed; if it never lands, set messageDelivered=false and log so the deacon backstop / existing kickoff machinery re-sends — never record a phantom delivery (the PAN-1491 failure). Keep the existing waitForReadySignal gate and the pi/codex branches unchanged.

subItems.title: Before delivering the auto-continue prompt on resume, a 'before' transcript snapshot is captured via the shared helper.
After delivery, the code polls (bounded) for a new user record; if none lands, it redelivers the same message exactly once and polls again.

messageDelivered=true and markKickoffRedelivered() are set ONLY after a landed user record is confirmed; if landing never confirms, messageDelivered=false and the failure is logged (no phantom success).
The pi and codex resume branches and the existing waitForReadySignal gate are unchanged.

Tests (using vi.useFakeTimers for the bounded poll/redelivery) assert: landing-on-first-delivery → no redelivery + messageDelivered true; never-lands → exactly one redelivery + messageDelivered false; lands-after-redelivery → messageDelivered true and markKickoffRedelivered called once when redeliveringKickoff.
typecheck, lint, and the resume tests pass.

items.title: Deacon backstop: re-send the continue prompt to a work agent stalled since resume with zero user records

narrative.Action: Add a new deacon patrol in src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts (model it on nudgeIdleWorkAgentsWithOpenBeads, deacon.ts:5899) and register it in runPatrol (deacon.ts:4818). For each running work agent with a live tmux session that is idle (isAgentIdleForNudge), detect a stalled resume: zero user records appended to the session JSONL since the last resume (via the shared transcript-landing helper, keyed off the resume entry the resume path already appends to continue state, or the agent's resume timestamp). When stalled, re-send the continue prompt (the default 'You are resuming work on <ISSUE>…' message, or the redelivered kickoff if kickoffDelivered===false), bounded by a per-agent cooldown file (mirror the .last-bead-nudge pattern). Do not fire for orphans (handled by recoverOrphanedAgents), closed issues, paused/troubled agents, or agents that already have user records since resume.

subItems.title: A new patrol identifies a stalled resume: status=running, role=work, live tmux, isAgentIdleForNudge, and ZERO user records in the session JSONL since the agent's last resume.
On detection it re-sends the continue prompt (not a generic ping), using the redelivered kickoff prompt when kickoffDelivered===false, otherwise the default resume continue message.
A per-agent cooldown (own marker file) prevents re-sending more than once per cooldown window; an agent with ANY user record since resume is skipped.
Orphans (no live tmux), closed issues, and paused/troubled agents are skipped — consistent with the existing patrol's gating.
The patrol is registered in runPatrol and its actions are logged like the sibling patrols.

Tests assert: a zero-user-record idle resumed agent is re-sent once then cooled down; an agent with a user record since resume is never re-sent; an orphan/closed/paused agent is skipped. typecheck + lint pass.
